tweets.text:
BREAKING: DHS and USCIS are ending Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Somalia. 

Temporary means TEMPORARY. 

Somali nationals with TPS are now required to leave the United States by March 17, 2026.
https://t.co/kTBIaf4KlW
tweets.text_resolved:
BREAKING: DHS and USCIS are ending Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Somalia. 

Temporary means TEMPORARY. 

Somali nationals with TPS are now required to leave the United States by March 17, 2026.
https://www.foxnews.com/politics/trump-administration-ends-temporary-protected-status-thousands-somalis-us

tweets.text:
Under the direction of @Sec_Noem, @DHSgov posted a Federal Register notice terminating the designation of Somalia for Temporary Protected Status, effective March 17, 2026. 

Temporary does not mean permanent!

To learn more, visit https://t.co/2UTned9Axt

Aliens should use the @CBP Home app to report their departure from the U.S.: https://t.co/wxxDjW4l3Y
tweets.text_resolved:
Under the direction of @Sec_Noem, @DHSgov posted a Federal Register notice terminating the designation of Somalia for Temporary Protected Status, effective March 17, 2026. 

Temporary does not mean permanent!

To learn more, visit https://www.uscis.gov/newsroom/news-releases/homeland-security-terminates-somalias-temporary-protected-status-designation

Aliens should use the @CBP Home app to report their departure from the U.S.: https://www.cbp.gov/about/mobile-apps-directory/cbphome

tweets.text:
Background checks from our Detroit office revealed that an alien from Bangladesh with pending felony robbery charges had been released. 

These charges qualify aliens to be detained under the Laken Riley Act, one of the first laws passed by President Trump at the beginning of his second administration. 

We immediately notified @ICEgov, who took the suspect into custody without incident.
tweets.text_resolved:

We immediately notified @ICEgov, who took the suspect into custody without incident.

tweets.text:
While conducting background checks, our team in Fresno found a criminal alien, who entered the US illegally, with a troubling record, including multiple arrests for beating his wife, driving drunk, using a false identity, imprisoning someone against their will, driving without a license, and disturbing the peace. 

We will not allow illegal alien criminals to stay in our communities.

tweets.text:
R-1 religious workers no longer need to reside and be physically present outside the United States for one year at the end of their statutory five-year maximum admission period of stay to be eligible for readmission in R-1status.

This improvement will promote stability and minimize disruptions to the essential services provided by nonimmigrant religious workers in the United States.

Learn more: https://t.co/PNcEvu9LRM
tweets.text_resolved:
R-1 religious workers no longer need to reside and be physically present outside the United States for one year at the end of their statutory five-year maximum admission period of stay to be eligible for readmission in R-1status.

This improvement will promote stability and minimize disruptions to the essential services provided by nonimmigrant religious workers in the United States.

Learn more: https://www.uscis.gov/newsroom/news-releases/dhs-reduces-wait-times-for-thousands-of-religious-workers-abroad

tweets.text: When our Milwaukee office discovered a Canadian alien with a pending Green Card application had recently been charged in Wisconsin with drunk driving and resisting arrest, causing injury to an officer, we coordinated with @ICEgov, and he was arrested at his adjustment of status interview.

tweets.text:
.@ICEgov apprehended a Nigerian national at our Houston office for his overstay in the U.S. and suspected marriage fraud. 

In addition, the criminal alien had multiple arrests for criminal mischief, trespass, failure to provide information, and falsely claiming U.S. citizenship. 

We only have room in the U.S. for law abiding applicants who will support and improve our great nation.

tweets.text:
In 1996, an immigration judge denied a Chinese alien’s asylum claim and ordered him to leave the United States. 

He remained in our country illegally, but @ICEgov will finally get to deport him after being arrested at our West Palm Beach office. 

Applying for U.S. citizenship cannot have a murky path. When you have been lawfully denied multiple times, you can’t keep filing new applications.
tweets.text_resolved:
In 1996, an immigration judge denied a Chinese alien’s asylum claim and ordered him to leave the United States. 

He remained in our country illegally, but @ICEgov will finally get to deport him after being arrested at our West Palm Beach office. 

Applying for U.S. citizenship cannot have a murky path. When you have been lawfully denied multiple times, you can’t keep filing new applications.

tweets.text:
Operation PARRIS is fully underway. We've surged USCIS resources to Minnesota to reexamine thousands of refugee cases through new background checks and intensive verification of refugee claims. We are working closely with @ICEgov to remove all criminal aliens guilty of fraud and other crimes from our communities.

This is just the beginning. Minnesota is ground zero for the war on fraud and this sweeping initiative will provide maximum vetting of all aliens, root out fraud, and ensure the safety of ALL Americans.

Learn more: https://t.co/geYQojzls1
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
USCIS is prioritizing the integrity of the VAWA program as a resource for women and other alien survivors of abuse. When unqualified aliens misuse the VAWA program, it causes significant processing delays, harming survivors with legitimate claims.

By clarifying the policies and requirements for aliens filing VAWA self-petitions, we are better equipped to combat fraud and manage the VAWA program as intended by Congress. Learn more:
https://t.co/tV6tqViKHh
tweets.text_resolved:
USCIS is prioritizing the integrity of the VAWA program as a resource for women and other alien survivors of abuse. When unqualified aliens misuse the VAWA program, it causes significant processing delays, harming survivors with legitimate claims.

tweets.text:
We are updating the USCIS Policy Manual to clarify the confidentiality provisions found in 8 U.S.C. § 1367(a)(1) and (a)(2) for aliens applying under the VAWA, T, and U programs. USCIS is prioritizing the integrity of these programs to safeguard them as a resource for women and other alien victims of crime.

For more information, visit https://t.co/sylmuxPDiS

tweets.text:
All USCIS fees must be paid via an ACH debit transaction from a U.S. bank using Form G-1650, or a credit/debit card using Form G-1450. Failure to use accepted payment methods will result in rejection.  

These changes reduce the risks of fraud, lost payments, and theft.  

Learn more: https://t.co/A90sAFW3Vq

tweets.text:
When an alien from Mexico arrived at our Fort Myers office, @ICEgov arrested him on a removal order from 2016.

If you’re in the U.S. illegally, it’s time to go home.

Through the @CBP Home App, you can self-deport and receive:
✈️A complimentary plane ticket home

✅Forgiveness of unpaid fines for prior failure to depart
